mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates rising labor income that feeds directly into consumer spending and aggregate corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of months following month-over-month PAYEMS increases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month rise in the unemployment rate of more than thirty basis points signals a deterioration in labor market slack that precedes downward revisions to household income and consumption forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3pp over the prior 3 months during a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales demonstrate a coordinated demand-supply expansion where factory output is absorbed by consumer spending, amplifying corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in INDPRO and RSAFS are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of coordinated upturns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below one percent, an increase in housing starts signals a credit-sensitive construction and durable goods cycle that transmits multiplier effects through the economy, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Increases in HOUST when FEDFUNDS is below 1% coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals broad-based demand strength that expands the earnings base for the aggregate corporate sector, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth surprises are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of outturns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production captures a manufacturing sector inflection that rapidly revalues cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO accelerations coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a suppressed federal funds rate and elevated headline inflation creates a negative real yield environment that forces capital out of fixed income and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where FEDFUNDS is low relative to CPIAUCSL inflation produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding two percent signals an abrupt consumer demand shock that compresses revenue expectations across discretionary and staples sectors, dragging S&P 500 returns lower over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines exceeding 2% month-over-month are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
